Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Mar-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS JOHN DOE 1, JOHN DOE 2, JOHN DOE 3 MOTION TO ADMIT KELLY W.G. CLARK AS COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Appearances: Jeralyn Kay Bercaw Spradlin, Esq. of Spradlin & Ferguson, (408) 262-0136 appearing for Defendant Edna Dowell; Amber Rae, Esq. & Lisa A. Sapcoe, Esq. of Armstrong & Assoc. LLP (510) 433-1830 appearing for Plaintiffs; Nicholas Heidt, Esq. of Sedgwick Detert, Oran & Arnold LLP (415) 781-7900, appearing for National Foundation of Boy Scouts; Mark V. Boennighausen, Esq., Attorney at Law, (408) 998-8500 specially appearing specially appearing for Allen Ruby, Esq., representing Corp. of the President of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints, a Utah Corp. The Court vacates its tentative rulings and rules as follows: Off Calendar, matter to be raised before the Superior Court in Santa Clara County. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MOTION TO TRANSFER
Off Calendar Mar-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT EDNA DOWELL MOTION TO TRANSFER (change of venue) from San Francisco Superior Court, to Superior Court of Santa Clara County, State of California. Appearances: Jeralyn Kay Bercaw Spradlin, Esq. of Spradlin & Ferguson, (408) 262-0136 appearing for Defendant Edna Dowell; Amber Rae, Esq. & Lisa A. Sapcoe, Esq. of Armstrong & Assoc. LLP (510) 433-1830 appearing for Plaintiffs; Nicholas Heidt, Esq. of Sedgwick Detert, Oran & Arnold LLP (415) 781-7900, appearing for National Foundation of Boy Scouts; Mark V. Boennighausen, Esq., Attorney at Law, (408) 998-8500 specially appearing specially appearing for Allen Ruby, Esq., representing Corp. of the President of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints, a Utah Corp. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted. This personal injury action is transitory and not local. Defendant has right to have the case transferred to the County of her residence. Defendant has shown that no co-defendant resides in San Francisco. Incident did not occur in San Francisco. Case is transferred to Santa Clara County. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
